mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.01% as of 2008-07-01 indicates easing cycle underway
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit stress
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return in periods when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% as of 2008-07-01 approaching threshold
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return in periods when UNRATE > 5.5% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, triggering negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return following RSAFS declines > 1% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating below 1% annualized signals supply-side weakness and capacity utilization stress, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress and recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate falls below 1% annualized
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return when INDPRO growth < 1% annualized is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both declining simultaneously indicate synchronized demand collapse across residential and consumer sectors, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession probability spikes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1066K as of 2008-06-01 showing weakness
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously is not significantly more negative than single-series declines (interaction F-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declining month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously signals accelerating labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession fears intensify and consumer spending contracts.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when both PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously is not significantly more negative than single-series moves (interaction F-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In monetary tightening regimes (FEDFUNDS > 3%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% month-over-month) compresses real returns and forces further rate increases, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. In easing regimes (FEDFUNDS < 2%), the same inflation signal is neutral or positive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.01% as of 2008-07-01 indicates easing regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Direction of 60-day forward returns flips when FEDFUNDS transitions from > 3% to < 2% regime (regime interaction F-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window in tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 1% annualized combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals demand-side recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised sharply downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth annualized < 1% AND HOUST declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1066K as of 2008-06-01 showing deterioration
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return when both GDPC1 growth < 1% and HOUST declines > 3% is not significantly more negative than single-series signals (interaction F-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.
